_schema.description: Authoritative mapping from outlet hostname to canonical metadata. Single source of truth for outlet country/language/type plus tier/editorial_independence/bias_note (migrated from config/sources.json 2026-05-29). Country values match the canonical names in src/stages/_helpers.py:COUNTRY_ALIASES targets so they pass normalise_country() unchanged.
_schema.key_format: Top-level keys are normalised hostnames (lowercase, no leading 'www.'). Subdomain matches fall back to the parent domain when no exact entry is present (see src/outlet_registry.py:lookup_outlet).
_schema.type_vocabulary_notes: academic = universities, peer-reviewed journals, state public-health / scientific institutes. think_tank = policy advocacy / research institutions (member-funded, foundation-funded, or government-supervised) with explicit policy-output orientation (Atlantic Council, Brookings, ISW, SWP Berlin, OSW, Baker Institute, Arms Control Association). The distinction is the transparency layer the project surfaces.

163.com.bias_note: Major Chinese internet technology company (NASDAQ/HKEX-listed), portal subject to PRC content regulations

pbs.org.bias_note: US public broadcaster; federal CPB funding rescinded in 2025, CPB dissolved Jan 2026; member-station funded

politico.eu.bias_note: EU politics and policy from Brussels. Replaces EuroNews.

polsatnews.pl.bias_note: Polish 24-hour news channel of Grupa Polsat Plus, controlled by businessman Zygmunt Solorz.

portaltela.com.bias_note: Brazilian Portuguese-language general news portal aggregating politics, sports and entertainment

prachatai.com.bias_note: Independent Thai non-profit (2004) with a human-rights, free-speech and reform focus that mainstream Thai outlets avoid; reformist, pro-democracy; operates under real legal pressure.

pravda.com.ua.bias_note: Ukrainian online news outlet (Ukrainska Pravda), owned since 2021 by Tomáš Fiala (Dragon Capital)

prensa-latina.cu.bias_note: Cuban official state news agency (founded 1959); Latin American-focused wire with global bureaux

prothomalo.com.bias_note: Largest-circulation Bangladeshi daily (Mediastar). Independent, broadly centrist, periodically critical of the governing party; Bengali edition.

protocol.ua.bias_note: Ukrainian legal-services portal offering lawyer directory and free legal-question consultations.

publico.pt.bias_note: Portuguese national daily founded 1990; owned by Sonae conglomerate via Sonaecom sub-holding.

rbc.ru.bias_note: RBK Group (business news+TV+web) owned by Grigory Berezkin's ESN Group since 2017.

rbc.ua.bias_note: Ukrainian news agency (RBC-Ukraine), privately owned by Yosyp Pintus (UBT media group); legally separated from Russian RBC in 2014

reliefweb.int.bias_note: UN OCHA humanitarian crisis reports. Primary source for underreported crises.

renewablematter.eu.bias_note: Bilingual Italian-English magazine covering circular economy and ecological transition, led by journalist Emanuele Bompan.

repubblica.it.bias_note: Italian daily newspaper, published by GEDI Gruppo Editoriale (controlled by Exor/Elkann family)

rescue.org.bias_note: International Rescue Committee, US-chartered humanitarian NGO; mixed funding incl. US/UK government grants and private donors

reuters.com.bias_note: International wire service, owned by Thomson Reuters Corp (NYSE/TSX-listed); Reuters Trust Principles govern editorial independence

rfa.org.bias_note: US Agency for Global Media outlet (Radio Free Asia); statutory editorial firewall under active litigation (2025–2026)

rfi.fr.bias_note: Radio France Internationale, francophone global. Replaces Jeune Afrique.

rg.ru.bias_note: Official newspaper of the Government of Russia; publishes federal laws and state legal acts.

ria.ru.bias_note: Russian state news agency (RIA Novosti), operated by FGUP MIA Rossiya Segodnya, a federal state unitary enterprise

richmondregister.com.bias_note: Small US local daily covering Madison County, Kentucky; owned by CNHI (Community Newspaper Holdings) since 1999

rline.tv.bias_note: Television channel of the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF).

rt.com.bias_note: Russian state media, included for transparency

rte.ie.bias_note: Irish national public-service broadcaster (Raidió Teilifís Éireann), statutory semi-state body under the Broadcasting Act 2009

rtl.fr.bias_note: French commercial radio network owned by RTL Group (via Groupe M6); RTL Group majority-controlled by Bertelsmann.

rts.rs.bias_note: National public broadcaster of Serbia, funded by subscription fee and advertising.

rtv.rs.bias_note: Regional public broadcaster for Vojvodina province; programs in Serbian plus 6 minority languages.

sondakika.com.bias_note: Turkish breaking-news aggregator/portal; privately operated commercial news site

stcn.com.bias_note: Securities Times — Chinese state-owned financial daily supervised and organized by People's Daily Press; one of four official PRC securities newspapers.

stern.de.bias_note: German weekly news magazine published by Gruner + Jahr; subsidiary of RTL Deutschland (Bertelsmann group).

tass.ru.bias_note: Russian state news agency; Federal State Unitary Enterprise wholly owned and controlled by the Russian government.

telesurtv.net.bias_note: Pan-Latin American satellite network headquartered in Caracas, funded by Venezuelan, Cuban and Nicaraguan governments

terra.com.br.bias_note: Brazilian web portal owned by Telefonica Brasil (Vivo); offers news, entertainment and digital services

theconversation.com.bias_note: Academic experts writing for general public. Nonprofit, Creative Commons.

theguardian.com.bias_note: Owned by the Scott Trust Limited, a private foundation endowed to secure the paper's financial and editorial independence.

thehindu.com.bias_note: Indian national English daily owned by The Hindu Group / Kasturi & Sons Ltd (private, family-controlled).

theins.ru.bias_note: Russian-language investigative outlet founded 2013 by Roman Dobrokhotov; editorial office in Riga, banned in Russia.

thepaper.cn.bias_note: Digital outlet owned by Shanghai United Media Group, a state-owned conglomerate under Shanghai CCP committee

thetimes.co.uk.bias_note: UK national daily; owned by News UK, a subsidiary of News Corp (Murdoch family-controlled).

touteleurope.eu.bias_note: French EU-affairs site published by GIE Toute l'Europe (economic interest grouping); current members are the SGAE (French state EU affairs secretariat) and SNCF.

unfpa.org.bias_note: UN sexual-and-reproductive-health agency working across 150+ countries; funded by voluntary government contributions.

uol.com.br.bias_note: Brazilian news portal Universo Online operated by UOL Inc., subsidiary of Grupo Folha (private, Frias family).

urdunews.com.bias_note: Urdu-language outlet owned by Saudi Research and Marketing Group (SRMG); launched in Jeddah 1994

usnews.com.bias_note: US digital news and rankings publisher privately owned by Mortimer Zuckerman since 1984
